testcases.input: towers = [[1,2,5],[2,1,7],[3,1,9]], radius = 2 Output: [2,1] Explanation: At coordinate (2, 1) the total quality is 13. - Quality of 7 from (2, 1) results in ⌊7 / (1 + sqrt(0)⌋ = ⌊7⌋ = 7 - Quality of 5 from (1, 2) results in ⌊5 / (1 + sqrt(2)⌋ = ⌊2.07⌋ = 2 - Quality of 9 from (3, 1) results in ⌊9 / (1 + sqrt(1)⌋ = ⌊4.5⌋ = 4 No other coordinate has a higher network quality.
testcases.expectedOutput: [2,1] Explanation: At coordinate (2, 1) the total quality is 13. - Quality of 7 from (2, 1) results in ⌊7 / (1 + sqrt(0)⌋ = ⌊7⌋ = 7 - Quality of 5 from (1, 2) results in ⌊5 / (1 + sqrt(2)⌋ = ⌊2.07⌋ = 2 - Quality of 9 from (3, 1) results in ⌊9 / (1 + sqrt(1)⌋ = ⌊4.5⌋ = 4 No other coordinate has a higher network quality.

testcases.input: towers = [[23,11,21]], radius = 9 Output: [23,11] Explanation: Since there is only one tower, the network quality is highest right at the tower's location.
